name: Korean commerce product review
summary: Reviews Korean shopping and checkout flows for fast phone input, explicit consent, payment clarity, catalog density, and accessible recovery.

criteria.title: Give recipient phone fields native input semantics
criteria.question: Can shoppers enter and autofill recipient phone numbers with the correct mobile keyboard?
criteria.evidence: Inspect phone-like inputs for type=tel and autocomplete=tel.
verification: Test recipient and purchaser phone fields with mobile keyboard and autofill.
falsePositiveNotes: Exclude order numbers, tracking numbers, and one-time-code fields.

criteria.title: Keep promotional consent unchecked
criteria.question: Are promotional messages separate from purchase terms and off by default?
criteria.evidence: Inspect checked checkboxes whose associated label mentions marketing, advertising, or promotional messages.
verification: Open checkout in a fresh session and confirm promotional consent is optional, separate, and unchecked.
falsePositiveNotes: Do not treat an account preference summary as a new checkout opt-in.

criteria.title: Make the checkout commitment explicit
criteria.question: Can the shopper see item total, delivery fee, discounts, final amount, payment method, and cancellation terms before purchase?
criteria.evidence: Capture the final checkout state and purchase confirmation with every amount and term visible.
verification: Review normal, sold-out, payment failure, retry, cancellation, and refund paths.
falsePositiveNotes: Optional business-only tax fields should not be required for consumer checkout.

criteria.title: Preserve catalog and order density without hiding decisions
criteria.question: Do price, discount, delivery, option, stock, and action information remain scannable at mobile and desktop widths?
criteria.evidence: Capture product list, detail, cart, and order-history surfaces at both declared viewports.
verification: Check Korean wrapping, price alignment, sticky actions, overflow, focus, and 44px touch targets.
falsePositiveNotes: A dense catalog can be appropriate when hierarchy and touch targets remain clear.

criteria.title: Prove checkout and recovery with assistive technology
criteria.question: Can keyboard and screen-reader users select options, edit cart items, recover from errors, and confirm purchase?
criteria.evidence: Attach keyboard order, accessibility-tree, error announcement, contrast, and focus-restoration evidence.
verification: Run the approved browser verification profile at mobile and desktop viewports.
falsePositiveNotes: Static labels do not prove dynamic price announcements or cart focus restoration.
The fixture intentionally preselects promotional consent and omits phone semantics; corrected controls must not emit either confirmed finding.

notes: The pack reviews supplied evidence only. It does not place orders, contact payment providers, or determine legal compliance.
